summary: Iteration 3 — Full backend + frontend regression for Stripe payments, People Intel module, and lead monetization gating. Backend: 36/36 pytest tests pass (10 new tests added: payments packages shape, checkout invalid pkg→400, checkout creates real Stripe session+pending txn and unpaid status keeps credits at 0, normal user sees scraped leads locked with blanked email/phone/source_url, admin sees them unlocked, unlock 402 without credits, unlock idempotency, people-intel scan deepseek with identity/footprint/risk, people-intel scan qwen graceful, people-intel history). Frontend Playwright: admin login OK, sidebar shows nav-people and nav-billing, topbar-credits shows '0 credits', Billing page renders 3 cards (pkg-starter $29/10cr, pkg-pro $99/50cr, pkg-agency $299/200cr) with Buy Now buttons; clicking buy-starter navigates to checkout.stripe.com (real Stripe test session). People Intel runs scan for torvalds and renders pi-report with AI profile (55% conf), Software Development/Online Discourse interests, 3 live accounts (x.com, github.com, tiktok.com all with 85% confidence), 1 public record, and RISK: LOW badge. Lead Engine shows 0 unlock buttons for admin (unlocked correctly). Full regression OK: Overview, Lead Scrapers, OSINT, NEXUS AI, API Keys, Admin, Logout all navigable. HF 403 → AI graceful (expected, not a bug).
critical_code_review_comments: /api/leads/{id}/unlock — when user is admin, the admin path skips the credits check AND skips $inc credits (good), but still calls $addToSet unlocked_by. That's fine semantically but means admin 'unlocks' are recorded as if a credit was used; consider not adding admin to unlocked_by to keep audit trail clean (or add a flag is_admin_unlock).

_settle_payment uses 'payment_status != paid' guard to prevent double-credit — correct idempotency. However if Stripe sends a 'paid' event twice and the first crashed before update_one, credits could still double-grant. Safer pattern: $set payment_status:'paid' as a conditional update with filter {payment_status: {$ne: 'paid'}} and only grant credits if matched_count==1.
/api/payments/status/{session_id} has no ownership check — any logged-in user can poll any session_id. Add filter {user_id: user['id']} when reading payment_transactions and reject if not owner.

/api/payments/checkout origin_url is taken straight from client; trust boundary: an attacker could supply an open-redirect domain in success_url. Validate origin_url against an allowlist (or your REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL host) before composing success_url.

people-intel scan stores `by: user_id` but /history returns scans across all users (no user filter). Consider scoping history to the requesting user, OR documenting it as a shared audit feed (admin-only).
_resolve_model still defaults to deepseek silently for unknown model values (carried from prior iteration) — minor.
test_credentials: admin@nexus.io / nexus123; non-admin via POST /api/auth/register with test_<uuid>@example.com.
seed_data_creation: Tests create throwaway @example.com users + a Stripe test-mode checkout session per run (left pending, no actual charge). A real Stripe test session was created at checkout.stripe.com/c/pay/cs_test_... — not completed, stays pending. People Intel runs persist 2 reports into db.people_reports (torvalds/deepseek + torvalds/qwen).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — 36 tests, ~12s. New iteration-3 tests cover Stripe (3), monetization gate (4), people-intel (3). HF_TOKEN 403 is expected → AI returns graceful error with HTTP 200. Stripe test key sk_test_emergent — checkout creates real test sessions; do NOT poll status expecting 'paid' since tests never complete payment. The unlock idempotency test promotes the user to admin briefly to bypass credit requirement (since there's no admin endpoint to grant credits directly — only paying does it). If you need to test the credit-decrement path end-to-end, you'd need either a payment_transactions seeder or a direct mongo write. The frontend pi-report data-testid is on a container that exists in both empty and populated states — waiting on substantive text content (e.g. 'github' or risk badge) is more reliable than wait_for_selector alone.
rca of the issue: No new bugs. Initial frontend script had a Python f-string backslash issue (cosmetic test bug, fixed). Initial People Intel test failed because data-testid='pi-report' selector matched the empty-state container — fixed by waiting for content to grow past placeholder.
